message:
Welcome to Once Upon a Time in Production, the responsibility is on you to size up the problem.

*Access game links by pressing the Uptime Labs Logo in the top right of the screen*
*Use `/incident` to create an incident record*

Once you're ready just click start to begin
Hey Everyone just in, customers are reporting the site images are not appearing, is this true?.
Just checked and all looks good for me
:star: Attempt to replicate the issue
I'm still seeing some product images missing, like the mug and sunglasses, so it might be intermittent.
i'm also able to purchase
Customers can technically check out, but we're seeing a 15-20% drop in conversions since the image issue started.
yep i can't see some items now too
checking reports, let me get back to you on what customers are saying
:star: Reproduces the incident
creating an incident
*Incident Number INC1018:* P2 15-20% drop in conversions
From what we can tell, the site isn't fully down. Customers can load pages and navigate categories, but something feels off visually
When did this start?
A few people mentioned pages look “incomplete” or “not finished loading”
any releases that could have caused this? shay
We're getting multiple reports of missing product images, broken icons, and some customers say the site looks unprofessional or untrustworthy.
Some customers are confused and think products are out of stock because images aren't loading.
Which regions are affected and how many customers/orders does this impact in real terms?
:star: Asking questions to validate impact of the problem
tanya can you also have a look too
:star: Check changes done to the platform
Checking recent changes...
Which regions are affected and how many customers/orders does this impact in real terms?
I did deploy a chaos test for ProductCatalogueService earlier.
But that's UAT only — nothing in prod.
Also added some extra logging in checkout.
Just log fields, shouldn't affect performance.
I pushed a small frontend UI tweak earlier.
Loading animation + accessibility stuff.
No backend or infra changes.
Checking application logs now…
What is the downside of rolling back those 2 changes to rule out?
bob Which regions are affected and how many customers/orders does this impact in real terms?
Seeing a huge spike in requests hitting ProductCatalogueService.
getting that info now
WARN [ProductCatalogueService] Request timed out after 3000ms path=/productCatalogue method=GET
Seeing slow dependency calls too.
WARN [ProductCatalogueService] Slow response from downstream dependency latency=1800ms
Customers must be spamming the products
can we scale anything up ?
PCS pod CPU is extremely high right now.
Service isn't crashing, but it's clearly struggling to keep up.
Looks to be a global issue
Checking infra now, give me a moment.
How many customers or orders does this impact? bob
Looks like it's a global issue
Seeing a lot more traffic reaching backend services than usual.
PCS request rate is way above baseline.
Been doing a comparison with Checkout and Frontend.
And they seem fine, let me show you
maya anything odd you can see?
https://i.ibb.co/fVY8zWQj/Checkout.png
https://i.ibb.co/Kk5gHqt/Frontend.png
https://i.ibb.co/chz2PJSH/PCS.png
PCS is the clear outlier.
I think all users can see this impact
Might be worth looping in Security to rule out something external.
already tagged maya
We're seeing reports from all regions, but the majority are coming from US and EU customers.
Thanks bob
Based on support tickets and analytics, about 30% of active users are affected, and order volume is down by roughly 15% since the issue began.
Rolling back my changes would remove the new checkout logging, but shouldn't impact core functionality or image delivery.
Rolling back my frontend tweaks would just revert the loading animation and accessibility improvements—no risk to backend or image paths.
We're seeing missing images and broken icons reported by customers in all regions, but most complaints are from the US and EU.
Upgrading severity to P1
*Incident Number INC1006:* P1
About 30% of active users are affected, and order volume has dropped by around 15% since the issue started.
Understood—please update me as soon as you have concrete numbers and regional scope.
We can scale PCS pods up, but the traffic spike is so extreme that it might just shift the bottleneck or increase costs without solving the root cause.
We're definitely seeing a lot of confused customers refreshing the page or trying to reload products, which could be amplifying the traffic spike.
double checking reports....
:star: Qualify the problem
About 30% of active users are affected, and order volume has dropped by around 15% since the issue started.
Getting over 100 reports so far on this
Looks like the common thread is that product images are not loading, and customers are seeing broken image icons instead. This is consistent across browsers
hamed Anything you recommend to do?
Definitely a global issue
Looking at the alerts now.
maya still waiting to hear back from you
Thank you maya
Seeing a big spike hitting image-related endpoints.
WAF rate-limit rules are firing.
ALERT: image-endpoint-rate-limit threshold=4,000/5min observed=15,000 action=BLOCK
Does the traffic look genuine?
ALERT: anomalous-request-rate endpoint=/productCatalogue status=triggered
don't want to block out genuine custoemrs
I'm calling this for now — looks like we're under a DDoS attack.
Before we start mitigation please update the business
WAF is blocking a lot of traffic, but the product catalogue service is still getting hammered—this is definitely a DDoS attack.
hamed do you reccomend scaling PCS?
Please update the business that we're under a DDoS attack and I'll prepare mitigation steps.
What can we do to stop the attack?
Let me send over some information to you.
How many customers and orders are currently impacted by this global P1 incident?
:star: Use metrics as evidence
:star: Manage actions and timings
I've been in contact with Hamed—he's currently out of office but should be available very shortly.
This document outlines the difference between DoS and DDoS attacks, as well as the mitigation strategies for each.
https://uptime-labs.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/ULPUBLIC/pages/769163265/DoS+V+DDoS+Attack+Understanding+the+Difference
I've shared the document with mitigation strategies—let me know if you want me to start blocking the most aggressive IPs first.
Checking if the traffic is legit...
So the IPs don't show typical attack patterns
What I see is legitimate user traffic behaving badly probably because something upstream isn't working as expected. The WAF is reacting to the volume, not intent.
The traffic seems legit
Im now confident that this is not a DDOS attack
Sorry for the delay, i just got in can you fill me in on whats going on?
Can you tell me what is causing the PCS to struggle, what is it getting requests for?
Before scaling, can you clarify what kind of requests PCS is struggling with?
Okay so we need to fix the image issue, customers are panicaking and simulating a DDoS
Wait images are served from S3 through CloudFront. PCS was the old path for images
Oh thats right i forgot PCS just returns the image URLs.
Yes thats right :cold_face: we moved images out of PCS a while back.
What is the estimated time to resolution and are we currently losing revenue every minute this continues?
If images aren't coming from S3, that could explain the load on PCS.
Okay what are the steps to resolve this
Worth checking S3 / CloudFront access.
and can someone give me an ETA for a fix?
First, check if S3 and CloudFront are accessible and serving images as expected.
Once we confirm S3 and CloudFront are working, images should recover quickly—maybe 10-15 minutes if it's just a config issue.
maybe 10-15 minutes if it's just a config issue.
i'll keep you updated
Thank you
Let us know if you need a status update or want us to escalate further.
status update please
We're investigating S3 and CloudFront access for image delivery; will update as soon as we confirm the root cause.
Okay who's taking the action on confirming S3 and CloudFront are working
Checking S3 / CloudFront access now.
Thank you—keep me posted with any changes or if the ETA shifts.
I think I found something.
Image requests at the edge are failing.
ERROR Static asset request failed / status=403 / reason=ExpiredPolicy
CloudFront is rejecting the image requests.
Looks like some kind of access policy or signing configuration has expired.
What's the fix for that
I'm not sure yet which part of the CloudFront setup is causing it.
When the edge can't serve images, the frontend falls back through ProductCatalogueService.
PCS was never scaled to handle that much traffic.
That explains the traffic spike and the WAF alerts.
Alright, thinking through fixes… give me a second.
Can you also include ETAs please
I see two paths we could take.
Option 1: scale up ProductCatalogueService so it can absorb the fallback image traffic.
That would stabilise the platform while images are still failing through CloudFront.
Option 2: restore the CloudFront access policy so images start loading from S3 again.
That fixes the root cause and removes the load from PCS.
Okay let's priorities the mitagation please,
Worth discussing before we commit.
:star: Take command
If we scale PCS, it could take 20-30 minutes to provision and test extra capacity.
Alright, if we go the scale route, here's the plan.
What are the downsides
First increase PCS replica count.
Then raise CPU and memory limits so autoscaling has headroom.
We'll monitor latency and error rates as traffic shifts.
I'll also adjust the WAF so legitimate traffic isn't blocked.
Scaling up ProductCatalogueService replicas now to handle the increased image traffic.
Alright, let's scale PCS for now. Post updates here.
Scaling PCS replicas now.
Before you give me the steps, i need to know which option get's us back up quicket
:star: Restoration over root cause analysis
Replica rollout complete.
Increasing CPU and memory limits.
PLEASE READ MY PREVIOUS MESSAGE
Easing WAF rate limits so legitimate traffic can pass.
Limits updated.
Done.
PCS latency is starting to improve.
Good sign. Let's keep monitoring.
Restoring the CloudFront access policy would get us back up fastest, likely within 5-10 minutes if there are no propagation issues.
Scaling PCS is safer if we're unsure about the CloudFront policy fix, but it takes longer—about 20-30 minutes to fully stabilise.
Restoring the CloudFront access policy is the fastest way to recover, with an estimated 5-10 minute turnaround if there are no propagation delays.
Can i get an ETA please
Scaling PCS is underway; we expect improved stability within 20-30 minutes.
Fixes are underway, getting you an ETA
I need confirmation as soon as revenue is restored and order flow returns to normal levels.
Scaling PCS is underway; we expect improved stability within 20-30 minutes.
I did not sign off on this option
Understood, pausing further scaling actions until we get explicit approval from Incident Manager.
i have asked for information
Apologies for moving ahead; to clarify, restoring the CloudFront access policy is the quickest fix (5-10 minutes), while scaling PCS internally is safer but slower (20-30 minutes).
which do you recommend, restoring the CloudFront access policy is the quickest fix (5-10 minutes), while scaling PCS internally is safer but slower (20-30 minutes).
The team are already underway with the safer option
Understood—keep me informed if the situation changes or if a faster fix becomes necessary.
What would be the downside of the quicker fix?
Restoring the CloudFront access policy could re-expose us to the original vulnerability, risking another wave of malicious traffic or abuse.
Will the faster option work?
Okay thank you, please go ahead with the scaling PCS
I'll check the platform
The images are appearing again for me
Images are visible for me!!
Not yet for me
PCS metrics show error rates are still a bit elevated, but trending down.
bob anything?
Customer reports are easing and the images are loading again across the site.
:star: Cross check resolution with stakeholders
Customer reports are easing and the images are loading again across the site.
:star: Confirm resolution with the business
Okay let's continue monitoring
Great work everyone, well handled.
Great work everyone
Indeed, for a while I was worried it was a security issue
Turns out it was a false alarm, not something you see every day
Can you confirm that order volume and revenue have fully recovered to pre-incident levels?
I remember Jamie had a similar issue not too long ago
Just done a test order and it went through fine
:star: Validate the fix
